conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture (generalization of 1.3 to bounded clique number)
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. IV. Consecutive holes
summary: The conjecture — that for all ν > 0 and k ≥ 3 there exists n such that every graph with no k-clique and chromatic number ≥ n contains ν consecutive hole lengths — remains open. The source paper (arXiv:1509.06563) proves the triangle-free case (k=3) but explicitly states no progress on the general bounded-clique-number extension. A wide web search found no subsequent paper resolving or substantially advancing the full conjecture; related progress (e.g. arXiv:1701.07217 on long odd holes in Kk-free graphs) concerns different hole properties and does not settle consecutive lengths for general k.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch confirms this paper is a tournament-structure survey (directed graphs, domination number, dichromatic number). It does not discuss consecutive holes, bounded clique number, or arXiv:1509.06563. The supplied contribution text is a mismatch — this reference is not relevant to the conjecture.

search_queries: consecutive holes bounded clique number chromatic number Scott Seymour induced subgraphs
arXiv 1509.06563 Scott Seymour consecutive holes clique number conjecture
consecutive hole lengths bounded clique number chromatic number graph theory 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up resolving the bounded-clique-number generalization was found in the indexed literature. The sole internal reference (arXiv:2306.02364) is about tournament structure and is unrelated to this conjecture. The conjecture is from 2018 and remains open with high confidence.
